newsSince: Incremental product news since June 9: Diodes launched several automotive power devices June 18-20 (AH3711Q Hall-effect latch, APK43070Q USB-C PD3.1 140W controller, DML1012ALDSQ 8mOhm smart load switch), extending its automotive power portfolio. Q2 guidance (~$435M revenue, +18.8% YoY) was set May 7. The +9.6% move since June 9 partly tracks this product cadence plus flow.

sources.label: DIOD expands automotive power portfolio (June 20) - Timothy Sykes
Diodes repositioning power portfolio around automotive - Simply Wall St

verdicts.rankRationale: On the AI-node bottleneck lens the June 18-20 product launches are automotive power, not AI/HBM/packaging - immaterial to this page. Prior remove case (weak direct AI-node relevance) stands; no AI-node evidence since June 9. Hold at the bottom.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Automotive power content growth - not AI-node relevant
verdicts.primaryRisk: No direct HBM/advanced-packaging/AI-networking exposure; broad cyclicality
verdicts.bull: New automotive power launches and +18.8% YoY Q2 guide show share gains in power management.
verdicts.neutral: Broad-line analog/discrete with auto momentum, but no direct AI compute-node bottleneck role.
verdicts.bear: Belongs off an AI-node alpha list; the new products are automotive, and +120% YTD worsens asymmetry.

verdicts.rankRationale: Through the co-packaged-optics lens the June automotive power launches have zero CPO relevance. No optical/data-center evidence since June 9; prior remove case (low direct CPO link, broad-cycle exposure) unchanged. Hold at the bottom.
verdicts.bull: Diversified discrete/analog franchise with automotive power momentum.
verdicts.neutral: Solid broad-line supplier but no co-packaged-optics content for this list.
verdicts.bear: Low direct CPO link plus cyclical end-markets; cleaner optical bottleneck names dominate.
